active_ingredients: Per caplet (e.g., Midol Complete): Acetaminophen 500 mg (pain reliever), Pamabrom 25 mg (a mild diuretic for water-weight gain/bloating), Pyrilamine maleate 15 mg (an antihistamine for irritability/cramps); some products substitute caffeine or ibuprofen as the analgesic
indications_and_usage: Uses: for the temporary relief of these symptoms associated with menstrual periods (premenstrual and menstrual): cramps, headache, backache, muscular aches, minor pain of arthritis, temporary fatigue, bloating/water-weight gain, breast tenderness, and irritability.
contraindications: Do not use if you have ever had an allergic reaction to any ingredient, with any other product containing acetaminophen or an antihistamine, with sedatives or tranquilizers, or in children under the age stated on the label.
warnings_and_cautions: Liver-damage warning - contains acetaminophen; do not exceed the maximum daily acetaminophen dose from all sources, and avoid heavy alcohol use (3+ drinks daily). May cause marked drowsiness from the antihistamine - do not drive or operate machinery; alcohol, sedatives, and tranquilizers may increase the drowsiness. Excitability may occur, especially in children. Ask a doctor before use if you have liver disease, glaucoma, an enlarged prostate/trouble urinating, or breathing problems (emphysema, chronic bronchitis). Stop use and ask a doctor if pain or fever lasts more than 10 days (pain) or 3 days (fever), new symptoms occur, or symptoms get worse. If you are pregnant or breastfeeding, ask a health professional before use.
adverse_reactions: Drowsiness, dry mouth, dizziness, increased urination, nausea; liver injury from acetaminophen overdose; rarely anticholinergic effects (blurred vision, constipation, urinary retention).
drug_interactions: Other acetaminophen-containing products (liver toxicity); alcohol and CNS depressants (additive sedation and liver risk); MAO inhibitors (prolonged/intensified anticholinergic effects); warfarin (regular acetaminophen use can raise INR); diuretics (additive fluid loss).
dosage_and_administration: Adults and children 12 years and over: take 2 caplets with water; may repeat every 6 hours as needed; do not exceed 6 caplets (3000 mg of acetaminophen) in 24 hours. Children under 12: do not use.
overdosage: Dominated by acetaminophen overdose (potentially fatal liver injury - early symptoms may be mild or absent; N-acetylcysteine is the antidote and is most effective early) and antihistamine overdose (anticholinergic toxicity, CNS depression). Seek emergency care; call Poison Control (1-800-222-1222) right away.
